user_brief: Create an original square logo for the open-source GitHub project 'ansys-aedt-mcp'. The project is a non-commercial Model Context Protocol server for Ansys Electronics Desktop automation through PyAEDT. Visual concept: electromagnetic field lines, circuit/mesh geometry, simulation nodes, and an MCP connector motif. Style: modern technical open-source repository mark, clean vector-like raster logo, high contrast, works on GitHub README light and dark themes. Avoid copying Ansys branding, avoid official trademarks, avoid dense text. Include at most a small readable 'AEDT MCP' mark if text is reliable.
research_queries: Ansys Electronics Desktop AEDT official overview PyAEDT
PyAEDT official documentation Ansys Developer Portal
Model Context Protocol official specification overview
GitHub Ansys open source organization repository AEDT Python Client Package

research_notes.point: AEDT is Ansys Electronics Desktop, a platform for electronics system design and access to EM simulation tools such as HFSS, Maxwell, Q3D Extractor, SIwave, and Icepak.
Ansys Developer documentation states AEDT supports ACT Python scripting and the PyAEDT and PyEDB libraries.
PyAEDT is the Python-facing automation layer for AEDT workflows, enabling simulation orchestration and data exchange.
MCP stands for Model Context Protocol; the spec is versioned and centered on a standardized protocol for tool/context interoperability.
Ansys maintains an open-source GitHub organization, so the project should visually fit an OSS repository identity rather than product packaging.

visual_strategy.use_case: Square GitHub repository avatar / README logo for a private, non-commercial open-source technical project.
visual_strategy.asset_type: Original vector-like raster logo mark with optional tiny wordmark.
visual_strategy.audience: Developers, engineers, and open-source users browsing a GitHub repository.
visual_strategy.core_message: A clean protocol bridge for automating electronics simulation workflows through a modern MCP server.

original_elements_to_preserve: Use the domain language of Ansys Electronics Desktop only as abstract cues: electromagnetic field lines, circuit traces, mesh grids, and simulation nodes.
Represent PyAEDT through automation/connector energy rather than any product UI or branded packaging.
Represent MCP as a universal protocol bridge, connector, or hub motif.
Keep the design clearly original and not visually adjacent to the Ansys wordmark, logo, or official color-block branding.
style_or_poster_language_to_reference: High-contrast readable silhouette at avatar scale
Abstract simulation-inspired motifs rather than literal screenshots
inferred_creative_additions: A central connector hub that feels like a protocol node linking multiple endpoints.
Radiating electromagnetic field arcs that double as a signal/network motif.
A compact mesh ring or polygonal lattice to evoke simulation geometry.
Three to five small nodes around the emblem to suggest distributed tools/services.
Optional tiny 'AEDT MCP' text only if perfectly legible and integrated unobtrusively.

image_prompt.primary_request: Create an original square logo for the GitHub project ansys-aedt-mcp: a modern technical open-source repository mark that combines a central MCP connector hub, electromagnetic field lines, circuit/mesh geometry, and a few simulation nodes into one compact, symmetrical emblem. It should feel like a clean software/project icon for an electronics simulation automation server, not a corporate brand.
image_prompt.scene_backdrop: Simple plain background or subtle transparent-like neutral field with no environment, no room, no desk, no UI screenshots, no product render; the emblem must stand alone as a logo.
image_prompt.subject: A bold central protocol bridge icon: interlocking connector shapes or a node hub at the center, surrounded by smooth electromagnetic arcs, mesh lattice lines, and a handful of small orbiting nodes that suggest simulation endpoints and automation flow.
image_prompt.composition_framing: Perfect square composition, centered icon, balanced symmetry, strong silhouette, generous negative space, logo-first framing, avatar-safe margins, readable at small sizes, no busy depth or perspective distortion.
image_prompt.style_medium: Clean vector-like raster logo, crisp edges, geometric abstraction, minimal but polished, subtle glow only if needed for separation, no photorealism, no 3D mockup, no heavy gradients, no clutter.
image_prompt.lighting_mood: High contrast, crisp, energetic, technical, slightly luminous field effect, suitable for both light and dark GitHub themes.
image_prompt.color_palette: Primarily monochrome or near-monochrome with one restrained electric accent such as cyan/teal or blue-violet; ensure excellent contrast on white and dark backgrounds; avoid Ansys corporate palette mimicry.
image_prompt.materials_textures: Flat or near-flat vector surfaces, very subtle tonal variation, clean linework, precise geometry, no grain, no noise, no paper texture, no chrome, no glass, no photoreal materials.
image_prompt.typography_layout: Prefer no text for maximum avatar clarity; if text is included, use only a tiny, highly legible 'AEDT MCP' mark integrated minimally beneath or beside the icon with generous spacing and no extra copy.
constraints: Original design only, not derivative of Ansys branding or any official product logo.
Square GitHub-friendly icon with a strong silhouette and clear center of focus.
Keep all text minimal and exact; avoid tiny unreadable lettering.
Use electronics simulation cues abstractly: field lines, mesh geometry, nodes, traces.
Make the MCP concept visible as a connector/bridge/hub, not as a generic cable.
Optimize for both light and dark backgrounds.
avoid: Ansys wordmark, logo shapes, official trademarks, corporate color-block imitation, or product UI replicas
Dense text, multiple lines of copy, tiny labels, unreadable typography, or random letters
Photorealistic rendering, glossy 3D mockups, or complex scene backgrounds
Overly literal USB plug/cable imagery that feels generic instead of protocol-specific
Messy composition, cluttered schematic details, low contrast, poor silhouette, or irrelevant symbols
Copying any existing emblem, badge, or brand mark associated with Ansys, AEDT, or MCP

final_generation_prompt: Design an original square GitHub logo for the open-source project 'ansys-aedt-mcp': a modern technical, high-contrast, vector-like raster emblem for a Model Context Protocol server that automates Ansys Electronics Desktop workflows through PyAEDT. Center a compact MCP connector hub or interlocking protocol bridge, wrapped with elegant electromagnetic field lines, circuit/mesh geometry, and 3–5 small simulation nodes to evoke electronics simulation and automation. Make the silhouette bold, symmetrical, and instantly legible at avatar size, with clean geometric linework and minimal detail. Use a monochrome or near-monochrome base with one restrained electric accent such as cyan/teal or blue-violet, optimized for both light and dark GitHub themes. Keep the background simple and uncluttered. Avoid any Ansys branding, official trademarks, corporate color-block mimicry, product screenshots, or generic cable/USB visuals. Prefer no text; if text is used, include only the exact small readable mark 'AEDT MCP' with perfect clarity and no additional words.
